title: Streams, Redirection, Quoting, and Pipelines · Operations Case: Stdin Stdout Stderr
summary: Build a repeatable incident-report pipeline whose data and diagnostics remain distinct and whose failures cannot be hidden by downstream success. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux stdin stdout stderr increment in a stream-routing and pipeline-trace workbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace standard input, standard output, standard error, file descriptors, and terminal attachment across a command.
Preview wildcard expansion and protect leading-hyphen, whitespace, newline, empty-match, and hidden-name cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:

# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback. Apply that move specifically to Stdin Stdout Stderr while producing a stream-routing and pipeline-trace workbook.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A 4-record incident command writes selected rows to report.txt and diagnostics to report.err. One grep stage finds no matches; predict descriptor routing and final status with pipefail before running. Competency probe: Trace standard input, standard output, standard error, file descriptors, and terminal attachment across a command. A defensible solution applies Stdin Stdout Stderr, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a stream-routing and pipeline-trace workbook.

content.code:
# Worked focus: linux-stdin-stdout-stderr — Trace standard input, standard output, standard error, file descriptors, and terminal attachment across a command.
set -o pipefail
grep -E '^INC-[0-9]+,high,' data/incidents.csv \
  >report.txt 2>report.err
status=$?
printf 'pipeline_status=%d\n' "$status"
content.caption: Runnable safe terminal evidence example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: All command output travels through one stream. This fails the Stdin Stdout Stderr contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
